commands.review_summary:
The branch adds an optional reconnect-delay delegate to `GatewayConnectionManager` and replaces fixed waits in `NodePairAutoApproveTests` with deterministic approval-completion synchronization.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection of current main shows the fixed `Task.Delay(200)` waits in the node auto-approve tests and the hard-coded one-second reconnect delay; I did not run tests because this is a read-only review.

target.title: [Repo Assist] test(connection): make node auto-approve tests deterministic
target.body:
🤖 *This is an automated PR from Repo Assist.*

## What

Replaces all `Task.Delay(200)` timing-based assertions in `NodePairAutoApproveTests` with deterministic synchronization primitives, and injects the 1 s post-approve reconnect delay so tests no longer wait on real wall-clock timers.

Closes #460

## Why / Root Cause

The tests had four `Task.Delay(200)` calls:

| Line | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| 77 | Assert no approval fired when `requestId == null` |
| 96 | Assert no approval fired when operator scope is insufficient |
| 123 | Wait for the duplicate-requestId CAS guard to run and reject |
| 127 | Wait for the first approval to complete after releasing the gate |

**Lines 77, 96, 123** are unnecessary: each of those handler paths executes synchronously (no `await` before the early return or CAS guard), so the assertion is safe the moment `FirePairingStatusChanged()` returns. The `Task.Delay` was just masking this.

**Line 127** needs a real signal. A new `WaitForApprovalCompleteAsync()` method on `TrackingGatewayClient` sets a TCS after `NodePairApproveAsync` returns, giving the test a precise "done" signal without any timer.

**Line 903 in `GatewayConnectionManager`** (`await Task.Delay(1000)`) lets the gateway process the approval before reconnecting the node. Tests can now inject `reconnectDelay: _ => Task.CompletedTask` to skip this entirely.

## Changes

- **`GatewayConnectionManager`**: add optional `Func<TimeSpan, Task>? reconnectDelay` constructor parameter (defaults to `Task.Delay`). Use it instead of the hardcoded `Task.Delay(1000)`.
- **`NodePairAutoApproveTests`**:
  - Pass `reconnectDelay: _ => Task.CompletedTask` in `CreateConnectedManager`.
  - Remove all four `Task.Delay(200)` calls.
  - Add `WaitForApprovalCompleteAsync()` to `TrackingGatewayClient` for the duplicate-request test.

## Trade-offs

The injected delay is purely a test-time concern. Production callers use the `Task.Delay` default and are unaffected.

## Test Status

✅ `dotnet test tests/OpenClaw.Connection.Tests/` — **229 passed, 0 failed**  
✅ `dotnet test tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests/` — 1928 passed (8 pre-existing failures unrelated to this change)  
✅ `dotnet test tests/OpenClaw.Tray.Tests/` — 1137 passed (10 pre-existing failures unrelated to this change)
